description:
You can configure `port_usages` and `networks` settings at the device level, but most of the time it's better use the Site Setting to achieve better consistency and be able to re-use the same settings across switches entries defined here will "replace" those defined in Site Setting/Network Template
In addition it is possible to use the `port_config_overwrite` to overwrite some attributes of the port_usage without having to create a new port_usage.

items.description:
ACL Policy:
  - for GBP-based policy, all src_tags and dst_tags have to be gbp-based
  - for ACL-based policy, `network` is required in either the source or destination so that we know where to attach the policy to

actions.description:
ACL Policy Actions:
  - for GBP-based policy, all src_tags and dst_tags have to be gbp-based
  - for ACL-based policy, `network` is required in either the source or destination so that we know where to attach the policy to

items.description: Action applied to traffic that matches a destination ACL tag

action.description: Policy action value that either allows or denies matching traffic. enum: `allow`, `deny`

dst_tag.description: Destination ACL tag matched by this policy action

name.description: Display name of the ACL policy

src_tags.description:
ACL Policy Source Tags:
  - for GBP-based policy, all src_tags and dst_tags have to be gbp-based
  - for ACL-based policy, `network` is required in either the source or destination so that we know where to attach the policy to

additionalProperties.description: Resource tags (`type`==`resource` or `type`==`gbp_resource`) can only be used in `dst_tags`

gbp_tag.description:
Required if
  - `type`==`dynamic_gbp` (gbp_tag received from RADIUS)
  - `type`==`gbp_resource`
  - `type`==`static_gbp` (applying gbp tag against matching conditions)

macs.description:
Required if 
- `type`==`mac`
- `type`==`static_gbp` if from matching mac

network.description:
If:
  * `type`==`mac` (optional. default is `any`)
  * `type`==`subnet` (optional. default is `any`)
  * `type`==`network`
  * `type`==`resource` (optional. default is `any`)
  * `type`==`static_gbp` if from matching network (vlan)

port_usage.description: Required if `type`==`port_usage`. Switch port usage name matched by this ACL tag

radius_group.description:
Required if:
  * `type`==`radius_group`
  * `type`==`static_gbp`
if from matching radius_group

specs.description: If `type`==`resource`, `type`==`radius_group`, `type`==`port_usage` or `type`==`gbp_resource`. Empty means unrestricted, i.e. any

items.description: Layer 4 protocol and destination-port match constraint for an ACL tag

protocol.description: `tcp` / `udp` / `icmp` / `icmp6` / `gre` / `any` / `:protocol_number`, `protocol_number` is between 1-254, default is `any` `protocol_number` is between 1-254

subnets.description:
If 
- `type`==`subnet` 
- `type`==`resource` (optional. default is `any`)
- `type`==`static_gbp` if from matching subnet

type.description:
enum: 
  * `any`: matching anything not identified
  * `dynamic_gbp`: from the gbp_tag received from RADIUS
  * `gbp_resource`: can only be used in `dst_tags`
  * `mac`
  * `network`
  * `port_usage`
  * `radius_group`
  * `resource`: can only be used in `dst_tags`
  * `static_gbp`: applying gbp tag against matching conditions
  * `subnet`'

acl_tags.description: ACL Tags to identify traffic source or destination. Key name is the tag name

additional_config_cmds.description: additional CLI commands to append to the generated Junos config. **Note**: no check is done

items.description: JUNOS "set" command to add to the generated configuration

additionalProperties.description: Aggregate route configuration for a network or routing instance

discard.description: Whether to install the aggregate route as a discard route

metric.description: Routing metric assigned to the aggregate route

preference.description: Route preference assigned to the aggregate route

aggregate_routes.description: Property key is the destination subnet (e.g. "172.16.3.0/24")

additionalProperties.description: Aggregate route configuration for a network or routing instance

discard.description: Whether to install the aggregate route as a discard route

metric.description: Routing metric assigned to the aggregate route

preference.description: Route preference assigned to the aggregate route

aggregate_routes6.description: Property key is the destination subnet (e.g. "2a02:1234:420a:10c9::/64")

additionalProperties.description: Switch BGP configuration for a routing instance

auth_key.description: Authentication key used for BGP neighbor sessions, when configured

bfd_minimum_interval.description: Minimum interval in milliseconds for BFD hello packets. A neighbor is considered failed when the device stops receiving replies after the specified interval. Value must be between 1 and 255000.

export_policy.description: Export policy must match one of the policy names defined in the `routing_policies` property.

hold_time.description: Hold time is three times the interval at which keepalive messages are sent. It indicates to the peer the length of time that it should consider the sender valid. Must be 0 or a number in the range 3-65535.

oneOf.description: BGP hold time value from 3 to 65535 seconds

import_policy.description: Import policy must match one of the policy names defined in the `routing_policies` property.

local_as.description: BGP AS, value in range 1-4294967294. Can be a Variable (e.g. `{{bgp_as}}` )

additionalProperties.description: Per-neighbor switch BGP session settings

disabled.description: If true, the BGP session to this neighbor will be administratively disabled/shutdown

export_policy.description: Export policy must match one of the policy names defined in the `routing_policies` property.

hold_time.description: Hold time is three times the interval at which keepalive messages are sent. It indicates to the peer the length of time that it should consider the sender valid. Must be 0 or a number in the range 3-65535.

oneOf.description: BGP hold time value from 3 to 65535 seconds

import_policy.description: Import policy must match one of the policy names defined in the `routing_policies` property.

multihop_ttl.description: Time-to-live value for multihop BGP sessions to this neighbor

neighbor_as.description: BGP AS, value in range 1-4294967294. Can be a Variable (e.g. `{{bgp_as}}` )

neighbors.description: Switch BGP neighbor settings keyed by neighbor IP address

networks.description: List of network names for BGP configuration. When a network is specified, a BGP group will be added to the VRF that network is part of.

type.description: BGP session type for this switch BGP configuration. enum: `external`, `internal`

bgp_config.description: BGP routing configuration for this switch. Property key is the BGP session name

created_time.description: When the object has been created, in epoch

default_port_usage.description: Port usage to assign to switch ports without any port usage assigned. Default: `default` to preserve default behavior

deviceprofile_id.description: Device profile associated with this switch

all_networks.description: Whether DHCP snooping applies to all configured networks

enable_arp_spoof_check.description: Enable for dynamic ARP inspection check

enable_ip_source_guard.description: Enable for check for forging source IP address

networks.description: If `all_networks`==`false`, list of network with DHCP snooping enabled

additionalProperties.description: DHCP server or relay configuration for one switch network. The property key is the network name. In case of DHCP relay, it's common for many networks to use the same dhcp relay, comma-separated network names can be used here (e.g. "net1,net2")

dns_servers.description: If `type`==`server` or `type6`==`server` - optional, if not defined, system one will be used

dns_suffix.description: If `type`==`server` or `type6`==`server` - optional, if not defined, system one will be used

additionalProperties.description: Static DHCP binding for a client MAC address

ip.description: Reserved IPv4 address for this fixed DHCP binding

ip6.description: Reserved IPv6 address for this fixed DHCP binding

name.description: Friendly name for this fixed DHCP binding

fixed_bindings.description: If `type`==`server` or `type6`==`server`. Property key is the MAC address. Format is `[0-9a-f]{12}` (e.g. "5684dae9ac8b")

gateway.description: If `type`==`server` - optional, `ip` will be used if not provided

ip_end.description: If `type`==`server`, ending IPv4 address for the DHCP lease pool

ip_end6.description: If `type6`==`server`, ending IPv6 address for the DHCP lease pool

ip_start.description: If `type`==`server`, starting IPv4 address for the DHCP lease pool

ip_start6.description: If `type6`==`server`, starting IPv6 address for the DHCP lease pool

lease_time.description: In seconds, lease time has to be between 3600 [1hr] - 604800 [1 week], default is 86400 [1 day]

additionalProperties.description: Custom DHCP option value

value.description: Option value to send for this DHCP option

options.description: If `type`==`server` or `type6`==`server`. Property key is the DHCP option number

server_id_override.description:
`server_id_override`==`true` means the device, when acts as DHCP relay and forwards DHCP responses from DHCP server to clients, 
should overwrite the Sever Identifier option (i.e. DHCP option 54) in DHCP responses with its own IP address.

additionalProperties.description: Vendor-encapsulated DHCP option value

value.description: Option value to send for this vendor option

vendor_encapsulated.description:
If `type`==`server` or `type6`==`server`. Property key is <enterprise number>:<sub option code>, with
  * enterprise number: 1-65535 (https://www.iana.org/assignments/enterprise-numbers/enterprise-numbers)
  * sub option code: 1-255, sub-option code'

dhcpd_config.description: Switch DHCP server or relay configuration keyed by network name

enabled.description: Whether switch DHCP server or relay configuration is enabled

disable_auto_config.description: This disables the default behavior of a cloud-ready switch/gateway being managed/configured by Mist. Setting this to `true` means you want to disable the default behavior and do not want the device to be Mist-managed.

dns_servers.description: Global dns settings. To keep compatibility, dns settings in `ip_config` and `oob_ip_config` will overwrite this setting

dns_suffix.description: Global dns settings. To keep compatibility, dns settings in `ip_config` and `oob_ip_config` will overwrite this setting

evpn_config.description: EVPN configuration settings applied to a Junos switch

enabled.description: Whether EVPN configuration is enabled on the switch

additionalProperties.description: IPv4 static route settings for a destination prefix

discard.description: Whether to install a discard route; this takes precedence over next-hop settings

metric.description: Route metric for the IPv4 static route

additionalProperties.description: Qualified next-hop attributes for an IPv4 static route

metric.description: Route metric for this qualified IPv4 next hop

preference.description: Route preference for this qualified IPv4 next hop

next_qualified.description: Qualified next-hop settings keyed by IPv4 next-hop address

no_resolve.description: Whether to prevent recursive next-hop resolution for the IPv4 static route

preference.description: Route preference for the IPv4 static route

via.description: Next-hop IP address. Can be a single IP address or an array of IP addresses for ECMP (Equal-Cost Multi-Path) load balancing across multiple next-hops.

extra_routes.description: Property key is the destination CIDR (e.g. "10.0.0.0/8")

additionalProperties.description: IPv6 static route settings for a destination prefix

discard.description: Whether to install a discard route; this takes precedence over next-hop settings

metric.description: Route metric for the IPv6 static route

additionalProperties.description: Qualified next-hop attributes for an IPv6 static route

metric.description: Route metric for this qualified IPv6 next hop

preference.description: Route preference for this qualified IPv6 next hop

next_qualified.description: Qualified next-hop settings keyed by IPv6 next-hop address

no_resolve.description: Whether to prevent recursive next-hop resolution for the IPv6 static route

preference.description: Route preference for the IPv6 static route

via.description: Next-hop IP address. Can be a single IP address or an array of IP addresses for ECMP (Equal-Cost Multi-Path) load balancing across multiple next-hops.

extra_routes6.description: Property key is the destination CIDR (e.g. "2a02:1234:420a:10c9::/64")

id.description: Unique ID of the object instance in the Mist Organization

image1_url.description: First custom image URL associated with the switch

image2_url.description: Second custom image URL associated with the switch

image3_url.description: Third custom image URL associated with the switch

additionalProperties.description: Switch IOT port configuration

alarm_class.description: Alarm class for the switch iot port in. enum: `minor`, `major`

enabled.description: Whether this switch IOT port is enabled

input_src.description: Only for "OUT" ports, input source for the switch iot port out. enum: `IN0`, `IN1`

name.description: Display name for the switch IOT port

iot_config.description:
Property Key is the IOT port name, e.g.: 
  * `IN0` or `IN1` for the FPC0 input port with 5V triggered inputs
  * `OUT1` for the FPC0 output port (can only be triggered by either IN0 or IN1)
  * "X/IN0`, `X/IN1` and `X/OUT` are used to define IOT ports on VC members

dns.description: Unique string values returned or accepted by this schema

dns_suffix.description: Unique string values returned or accepted by this schema

gateway.description: Default gateway IPv4 address for this Junos IP configuration

ip.description: Configured IPv4 address for this Junos IP configuration

netmask.description: Used only if `subnet` is not specified in `networks`

network.description: Management network for this IP configuration; used as the default source network for outbound SSH, DNS, NTP, TACACS+, RADIUS, syslog, and SNMP

type.description: IP address assignment mode, either DHCP or static. enum: `dhcp`, `static`

additionalProperties.description: Per-port Switch Port Operator (SPO) override configuration used in `local_port_config` to customize settings inherited from `port_config`

all_networks.description: Only if `mode`==`trunk` whether to trunk all network/vlans

allow_dhcpd.description: Controls whether DHCP server traffic is allowed on ports using this configuration if DHCP snooping is enabled. This is a tri-state setting; `true`: ports become trusted ports allowing DHCP server traffic, `false`: ports become untrusted blocking DHCP server traffic, undefined: use system defaults (access ports default to untrusted, trunk ports default to trusted).

allow_multiple_supplicants.description: Whether multiple supplicants may authenticate on the port

bypass_auth_when_server_down.description: Only if `port_auth`==`dot1x` bypass auth for known clients if set to true when RADIUS server is down

bypass_auth_when_server_down_for_unknown.description: Only if `port_auth`=`dot1x` bypass auth for all (including unknown clients) if set to true when RADIUS server is down

description.description: Human-readable description for this local port configuration

disable_autoneg.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` if speed and duplex are specified, whether to disable autonegotiation

dynamic_vlan_networks.description: Only if `port_auth`==`dot1x`, if dynamic vlan is used, specify the possible networks/vlans RADIUS can return

enable_mac_auth.description: Only if `port_auth`==`dot1x` whether to enable MAC Auth

enable_qos.description: Whether QoS is enabled on ports using this local configuration

guest_network.description: Only if `port_auth`==`dot1x` which network to put the device into if the device cannot do dot1x. default is null (i.e. not allowed)

inter_switch_link.description: Used together with "isolation" under networks for links between Juniper devices; must be applied to both connected ports

mac_auth_only.description: Only if `enable_mac_auth`==`true`, whether to use MAC authentication without 802.1X

mac_auth_preferred.description: Only if `enable_mac_auth`==`true` + `mac_auth_only`==`false`, dot1x will be given priority then mac_auth. Enable this to prefer mac_auth over dot1x.

mac_auth_protocol.description: Only if `enable_mac_auth` ==`true`. This type is ignored if mist_nac is enabled. enum: `eap-md5`, `eap-peap`, `pap`

mac_limit.description: Max number of MAC addresses, default is 0 for unlimited, otherwise range is 1 or higher, with upper bound constrained by platform

mtu.description: Media maximum transmission unit (MTU) is the largest data unit that can be forwarded without fragmentation. The default value is 1514.

networks.description: Only if `mode`==`trunk`, the list of network/vlans

note.description: Additional note for the port config override

persist_mac.description: Only if `mode`==`access` and `port_auth`!=`dot1x` whether the port should retain dynamically learned MAC addresses

poe_disabled.description: Whether PoE capabilities are disabled for a port

port_auth.description: if dot1x is desired, set to dot1x. enum: `dot1x`

reauth_interval.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`=`dot1x` reauthentication interval range (min: 10, max: 65535, default: 3600). Set to 0 to disable reauthentication (no-reauthentication).

server_fail_network.description: Only if `port_auth`==`dot1x` sets server fail fallback vlan

server_reject_network.description: Only if `port_auth`==`dot1x` when RADIUS server reject / fails

storm_control.description: Storm-control settings for this local port configuration

disable_port.description: Whether to disable the port when storm control is triggered

no_broadcast.description: Whether to disable storm control on broadcast traffic

no_multicast.description: Whether to disable storm control on multicast traffic

no_registered_multicast.description: Whether to disable storm control on registered multicast traffic

no_unknown_unicast.description: Whether to disable storm control on unknown unicast traffic

percentage.description: Bandwidth-percentage, configures the storm control level as a percentage of the available bandwidth

stp_edge.description: When enabled, the port is not expected to receive BPDU frames

stp_no_root_port.description: Whether STP should prevent this port from becoming a root port

stp_p2p.description: Whether STP treats this port as a point-to-point link

usage.description: Port usage profile name for this local port configuration

use_vstp.description: If this is connected to a vstp network

voip_network.description: Network/vlan for voip traffic, must also set port_network. to authenticate device, set port_auth

local_port_config.description: Per-port Switch Port Operator (SPO) override, overriding the port configuration from `port_config`. Property key is the port name or range (e.g. "ge-0/0/0-10")

mac.description: Switch MAC address used to identify the device

managed.description: An adopted switch/gateway will not be managed/configured by Mist by default. Setting this parameter to `true` enables the adopted switch/gateway to be managed/configured by Mist. Deprecated in favour of mist_configured, which is more intuitive and can be used for both adopted and claimed devices.

map_id.description: Map where the device belongs to

mist_configured.description: whether the device can be configured by Mist or not. This deprecates `managed` (for adopted device) and `disable_auto_config` for claimed device)

mist_nac.description: Mist NAC RadSec settings for a switch

enabled.description: Whether Mist NAC RadSec is enabled for the switch

network.description: Switch network used for Mist NAC RadSec connectivity

model.description: Switch model reported for the device

modified_time.description: When the object has been modified for the last time, in epoch

name.description: Friendly display name assigned to the switch

additionalProperties.description: A network represents a network segment. It can either represent a VLAN (then usually ties to a L3 subnet), optionally associate it with a subnet which can later be used to create addition routes. Used for ports doing `family ethernet-switching`. It can also be a pure L3-subnet that can then be used against a port that with `family inet`.

gateway.description: Only required for EVPN-VXLAN networks, IPv4 Virtual Gateway

gateway6.description: Only required for EVPN-VXLAN networks, IPv6 Virtual Gateway

isolation.description: whether to stop clients to talk to each other, default is false (when enabled, a unique isolation_vlan_id is required). NOTE: this features requires uplink device to also a be Juniper device and `inter_switch_link` to be set. See also `inter_isolation_network_link` and `community_vlan_id` in port_usage

isolation_vlan_id.description: Required when `isolation`==`true`. Unique VLAN ID used for client isolation

subnet.description: Optional for pure switching, required when L3 / routing features are used

subnet6.description: Optional for pure switching, required when L3 / routing features are used

vlan_id.description: VLAN ID, either numeric or expressed as a template variable string

notes.description: Free-form administrative notes for this switch

ntp_servers.description: List of NTP servers specific to this device. By default, those in Site Settings will be used

oob_ip_config.description:
Switch OOB IP Config:
  - If HA configuration: key parameter will be nodeX (eg: node1)
  - If there are 2 routing engines, re1 mgmt IP has to be set separately (if desired): key parameter = `re1`

gateway.description: Default gateway for the out-of-band management interface when `type`==`static`

ip.description: Static IPv4 address for the out-of-band management interface when `type`==`static`

netmask.description: Used only if `subnet` is not specified in `networks`

network.description: Optional, the network to be used for mgmt

type.description: IP address assignment mode, either DHCP or static. enum: `dhcp`, `static`

use_mgmt_vrf.description: If supported on the platform. If enabled, DNS will be using this routing-instance, too

use_mgmt_vrf_for_host_out.description: For host-out traffic (NTP/TACPLUS/RADIUS/SYSLOG/SNMP), if alternative source network/ip is desired

org_id.description: Unique identifier of a Mist organization

additionalProperties.description: Property key is the OSPF Area (Area should be a number (0-255) / IP address)

include_loopback.description: Whether loopback interfaces are included in this OSPF area

additionalProperties.description: Property key is the network name. Networks to participate in an OSPF area

auth_keys.description: Required if `auth_type`==`md5`. Property key is the key number

auth_password.description: Required if `auth_type`==`password`, the password, max length is 8

bfd_minimum_interval.description: Minimum BFD interval for this OSPF network, in milliseconds

dead_interval.description: OSPF dead interval for this network, in seconds

export_policy.description: Routing policy used to export routes from this OSPF network

hello_interval.description: OSPF hello interval for this network, in seconds

import_policy.description: Routing policy used to import routes for this OSPF network

metric.description: OSPF metric assigned to this network

no_readvertise_to_overlay.description: By default, we'll re-advertise all learned OSPF routes toward overlay

networks.description: OSPF network settings keyed by network name

ospf_areas.description: Junos OSPF areas. Property key is the OSPF Area (Area should be a number (0-255) / IP address)

ospf_config.description: OSPF routing configuration for a Junos switch

additionalProperties.description: Settings for a single OSPF area on a switch

no_summary.description: Disable OSPF summary routes for this area

areas.description: Property key is the area name. Defines the OSPF areas configured on the switch.

export_policy.description: optional, for basic scenario, `import_policy` can be specified and can be applied to all networks in all areas if not explicitly specified

import_policy.description: optional, for basic scenario, `import_policy` can be specified and can be applied to all networks in all areas if not explicitly specified

additionalProperties.description: Optional switch L3 presence on an additional network or VLAN

evpn_anycast.description: For EVPN, whether anycast is desired

ip.description: Required if `type`==`static`; IPv4 address for the additional Junos L3 presence

ip6.description: Required if `type6`==`static`; IPv6 address for the additional Junos L3 presence

netmask.description: Optional IPv4 netmask; `subnet` from `network` definition will be used if defined

netmask6.description: Optional IPv6 prefix length; `subnet` from `network` definition will be used if defined

type.description: IP address assignment mode, either DHCP or static. enum: `dhcp`, `static`

other_ip_configs.description: Property key is the network name. Defines the additional IP Addresses configured on the device.

additionalProperties.description: Junos switch port configuration

ae_disable_lacp.description: To disable LACP support for the AE interface

ae_idx.description: Users could force to use the designated AE name

ae_lacp_force_up.description: If `aggregated`==`true`, sets the state of the interface as UP when the peer has limited LACP capability. Use case: When a device connected to this AE port is ZTPing for the first time, it will not have LACP configured on the other end. **Note:** Turning this on will enable force-up on one of the interfaces in the bundle only

ae_lacp_passive.description: If `aggregated`==`true`, sets LACP to passive mode on this AE interface; by default, active (fast) mode is used

aggregated.description: Whether this port is configured as an aggregated Ethernet member

description.description: Human-readable description for this Junos port

disable_autoneg.description: If `speed` and `duplex` are specified, whether to disable autonegotiation

dynamic_usage.description: Enable dynamic usage for this port. Set to `dynamic` to enable.

esilag.description: Whether this Junos port participates in an ESI-LAG

mtu.description: Media maximum transmission unit (MTU) is the largest data unit that can be forwarded without fragmentation

networks.description: List of network names. Required if `usage`==`inet`

no_local_overwrite.description: Prevent helpdesk to override the port config

poe_disabled.description: Whether PoE capabilities are disabled for this Junos port

port_network.description: Required if `usage`==`vlan_tunnel`. Q-in-Q tunneling using All-in-one bundling. This also enables standard L2PT for interfaces that are not encapsulation tunnel interfaces and uses MAC rewrite operation. [View more information](https://www.juniper.net/documentation/us/en/software/junos/multicast-l2/topics/topic-map/q-in-q.html#id-understanding-qinq-tunneling-and-vlan-translation)

usage.description: Port usage name. For Q-in-Q, use `vlan_tunnel`. If EVPN is used, use `evpn_uplink`or `evpn_downlink`

port_config.description: Property key is the port name or range (e.g. "ge-0/0/0-10")

additionalProperties.description: Switch port configuration overrides

description.description: Administrative description applied to the switch port override

mac_limit.description: Max number of MAC addresses, default is 0 for unlimited, otherwise range is 1 to 16383 (upper bound constrained by platform)

poe_disabled.description: Whether PoE capabilities are disabled for a port

poe_keep_state_when_reboot.description: Whether Perpetual PoE is enabled; keeps PoE state across reboots

speed.description: Port Speed, default is auto to automatically negotiate speed enum: `100m`, `10m`, `1g`, `2.5g`, `5g`, `10g`, `25g`, `40g`, `100g`,`auto`

port_config_overwrite.description: Property key is the port name or range (e.g. "ge-0/0/0-10"). This can be used to override some attributes of the port_usage without having to create a new port_usage.

additionalProperties.description: Input and output settings for one switch port mirroring session

input_networks_ingress.description: At least one of the `input_port_ids_ingress`, `input_port_ids_egress` or `input_networks_ingress ` should be specified

input_port_ids_egress.description: At least one of the `input_port_ids_ingress`, `input_port_ids_egress` or `input_networks_ingress ` should be specified

input_port_ids_ingress.description: At least one of the `input_port_ids_ingress`, `input_port_ids_egress` or `input_networks_ingress ` should be specified

output_ip_address.description: Exactly one of the `output_ip_address`, `output_port_id` or `output_network` should be provided

output_network.description: Exactly one of the `output_ip_address`, `output_port_id` or `output_network` should be provided

output_port_id.description: Exactly one of the `output_ip_address`, `output_port_id` or `output_network` should be provided

port_mirroring.description: Property key is the port mirroring instance name. `port_mirroring` can be added under device/site settings. It takes interface and ports as input for ingress, interface as input for egress and can take interface and port as output. A maximum 4 mirroring ports is allowed

additionalProperties.description: Junos switch port usage template and authentication settings

all_networks.description: Only if `mode`==`trunk`. Whether to trunk all network/vlans

allow_dhcpd.description: Only applies when `mode`!=`dynamic`. Controls whether DHCP server traffic is allowed on ports using this configuration if DHCP snooping is enabled. This is a tri-state setting; `true`: ports become trusted ports allowing DHCP server traffic, `false`: ports become untrusted blocking DHCP server traffic, undefined: use system defaults (access ports default to untrusted, trunk ports default to trusted).

bypass_auth_when_server_down.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`==`dot1x`. Bypass auth for known clients if set to true when RADIUS server is down

bypass_auth_when_server_down_for_unknown.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`=`dot1x`. Bypass auth for all (including unknown clients) if set to true when RADIUS server is down

bypass_auth_when_server_down_for_voip.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`==`dot1x`. Bypass auth for VOIP if set to true when RADIUS server is down

community_vlan_id.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. To be used together with `isolation` under networks. Signaling that this port connects to the networks isolated but wired clients belong to the same community can talk to each other

disable_autoneg.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. If speed and duplex are specified, whether to disable autonegotiation

disabled.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. Whether the port is disabled

duplex.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. Link connection mode. enum: `auto`, `full`, `half`

dynamic_vlan_networks.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`==`dot1x`, if dynamic vlan is used, specify the possible networks/vlans RADIUS can return

enable_mac_auth.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`==`dot1x`. Whether to enable MAC Auth

guest_network.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`==`dot1x`. Which network to put the device into if the device cannot do dot1x. default is null (i.e. not allowed)

inter_isolation_network_link.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. `inter_isolation_network_link` is used together with `isolation` under networks, signaling that this port connects to isolated networks

inter_switch_link.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. `inter_switch_link` is used together with `isolation` under networks. NOTE: `inter_switch_link` works only between Juniper devices. This has to be applied to both ports connected together

mac_auth_preferred.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` + `enable_mac_auth`==`true` + `mac_auth_only`==`false`, dot1x will be given priority then mac_auth. Enable this to prefer mac_auth over dot1x.

mac_auth_protocol.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `enable_mac_auth` ==`true`. This type is ignored if mist_nac is enabled. enum: `eap-md5`, `eap-peap`, `pap`

mac_limit.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`, max number of MAC addresses, default is 0 for unlimited, otherwise range is 1 to 16383 (upper bound constrained by platform)

mode.description: `mode`==`dynamic` must only be used if the port usage name is `dynamic`. enum: `access`, `dynamic`, `inet`, `trunk`

mtu.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` media maximum transmission unit (MTU) is the largest data unit that can be forwarded without fragmentation. The default value is 1514.

networks.description: Only if `mode`==`trunk`, the list of network/vlans

persist_mac.description: Only if `mode`==`access` and `port_auth`!=`dot1x`. Whether the port should retain dynamically learned MAC addresses

poe_disabled.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. Whether PoE capabilities are disabled for a port

poe_keep_state_when_reboot.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. Whether Perpetual PoE is enabled; keeps PoE state across reboots

port_auth.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. If dot1x is desired, set to dot1x. enum: `dot1x`

port_network.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. Native network/vlan for untagged traffic

reauth_interval.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`=`dot1x` reauthentication interval range (min: 10, max: 65535, default: 3600). Set to 0 to disable reauthentication (no-reauthentication).

reset_default_when.description: Only if `mode`==`dynamic` Control when the DPC port should be changed to the default port usage. enum: `link_down`, `none` (let the DPC port keep at the current port usage)

rules.description: Only if `mode`==`dynamic`. Dynamic matching rules that select the port usage to apply

items.description: Dynamic port usage rule evaluated against LLDP, RADIUS, or peer MAC attributes

equals.description: Exact value that the selected source attribute must match

equals_any.description: Use `equals_any` to match any item in a list

usage.description: Port usage name to apply when this dynamic rule matches

server_fail_network.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`==`dot1x`. Sets server fail fallback vlan

server_fail_retry_interval.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`==`dot1x`. Interval, in seconds. Sets the wait time before retrying authentication after RADIUS failure to reduce client flapping. Range 120-65535

server_reject_network.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`==`dot1x`. When RADIUS server reject / fails

speed.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`, Port speed, default is auto to automatically negotiate speed enum: `100m`, `10m`, `1g`, `2.5g`, `5g`, `10g`, `25g`, `40g`, `100g`,`auto`

disable_port.description: Whether to disable the port when storm control is triggered

no_broadcast.description: Whether to disable storm control on broadcast traffic

no_multicast.description: Whether to disable storm control on multicast traffic

no_registered_multicast.description: Whether to disable storm control on registered multicast traffic

no_unknown_unicast.description: Whether to disable storm control on unknown unicast traffic

percentage.description: Bandwidth-percentage, configures the storm control level as a percentage of the available bandwidth

stp_disable.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `stp_required`==`false`. Drop bridge protocol data units (BPDUs ) that enter any interface or a specified interface

stp_edge.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. When enabled, the port is not expected to receive BPDU frames

stp_required.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. Whether to remain in block state if no BPDU is received

ui_evpntopo_id.description: Optional for Campus Fabric Core-Distribution ESI-LAG profile. Helper used by the UI to select this port profile as the ESI-Lag between Distribution and Access switches

use_vstp.description: If this is connected to a vstp network

voip_network.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. Network/vlan for voip traffic, must also set port_network. to authenticate device, set port_auth

port_usages.description: Property key is the port usage name. Defines the profiles of port configuration configured on the switch

radius_config.description: Switch RADIUS authentication and accounting configuration

acct_immediate_update.description: Whether immediate RADIUS accounting updates are sent

acct_interim_interval.description: How frequently should interim accounting be reported, 60-65535. default is 0 (use one specified in Access-Accept request from RADIUS Server). Very frequent messages can affect the performance of the RADIUS server, 600 and up is recommended when enabled

host.description: Address or hostname of the RADIUS accounting server

keywrap_enabled.description: Whether RADIUS keywrap is enabled for messages sent to this accounting server

keywrap_format.description: Encoding format for RADIUS keywrap KEK and MACK values. enum: `ascii`, `hex`

keywrap_mack.description: RADIUS keywrap message authentication code key (MACK)

port.description: RADIUS Auth Port, value from 1 to 65535, default is 1813

secret.description: Shared secret used with this RADIUS accounting server

auth_server_selection.description: Selection strategy for RADIUS authentication servers. enum: `ordered`, `unordered`

host.description: Address or hostname of the RADIUS authentication server

keywrap_enabled.description: Whether RADIUS keywrap is enabled for messages sent to this authentication server

keywrap_format.description: Encoding format for RADIUS keywrap KEK and MACK values. enum: `ascii`, `hex`

keywrap_mack.description: RADIUS keywrap message authentication code key (MACK)

port.description: RADIUS Auth Port, value from 1 to 65535, default is 1812

require_message_authenticator.description: Whether to require Message-Authenticator in requests

secret.description: Shared secret used with this RADIUS authentication server

coa_enabled.description: Whether RADIUS Change of Authorization (CoA) is enabled

coa_port.description: RADIUS CoA Port, value from 1 to 65535, default is 3799

fast_dot1x_timers.description: Whether fast 802.1X timers are enabled for RADIUS authentication

network.description: Use `network`or `source_ip`. Which network the RADIUS server resides, if there's static IP for this network, we'd use it as source-ip

source_ip.description: Use `network` or `source_ip`. Explicit source IP address for RADIUS traffic

files.description: Number of archived syslog files to retain

size.description: Maximum size of each archived syslog file, such as 5m

cacerts.description: CA certificates used to verify TLS syslog servers

console.description: Console log forwarding filters for remote syslog

items.description: Syslog message content selector for remote logging

enabled.description: Whether remote syslog forwarding is enabled

files.description: Number of archived syslog files to retain

size.description: Maximum size of each archived syslog file, such as 5m

items.description: Syslog message content selector for remote logging

enable_tls.description: Only if `protocol`==`tcp`, enable TLS for this syslog file destination

explicit_priority.description: Whether to include explicit syslog priority values in file output

match.description: Expression used to filter log messages written to this file

structured_data.description: Whether to include structured syslog data in file output

network.description: Source network used for syslog traffic. If `source_address` is configured, Mist uses the VLAN first; otherwise it uses `source_ip`

send_to_all_servers.description: Whether each log entry is sent to all configured remote syslog servers

items.description: Syslog message content selector for remote logging

explicit_priority.description: Whether to include explicit syslog priority values in messages sent to this server

host.description: Address or hostname of the remote syslog server

match.description: Expression used to filter log messages sent to this server

port.description: Syslog Service Port, value from 1 to 65535

protocol.description: Transport protocol used for this remote syslog server. enum: `tcp`, `udp`

routing_instance.description: Routing instance used to reach this remote syslog server

server_name.description: TLS server name used when verifying the remote syslog server certificate

source_address.description: Source address for syslog traffic. If configured, Mist uses the VLAN first; otherwise it uses `source_ip`

structured_data.description: Whether to include structured syslog data in messages sent to this server

tag.description: Syslog tag value added to messages sent to this server

items.description: Syslog message content selector for remote logging

match.description: Expression used to filter user log messages

user.description: Account name or wildcard matched by this syslog rule

role.description: Deployment role label for this switch

additionalProperties.description: Switch routing policy made of ordered match-action terms

terms.description: Ordered switch routing policy terms; at least one term is required

items.description: Switch routing policy term with match criteria and actions

actions.description: Actions applied to routes matched by a switch routing policy term

accept.description: Whether to accept routes that match this term

community.description: When used as export policy, optional

local_preference.description: Optional, for an import policy, local_preference can be changed, value in range 1-4294967294. Can be a Variable (e.g. `{{bgp_as}}`)

prepend_as_path.description: When used as export policy, optional. By default, the local AS will be prepended, to change it. Can be a Variable (e.g. `{{as_path}}`)

matching.description: Route match criteria for a switch routing policy term; all specified criteria must match

as_path.description: BGP AS path values used as routing-policy match criteria

items.description: BGP AS, value in range 1-4294967294. Can be a Variable (e.g. `{{bgp_as}}` )

community.description: BGP community values used as routing-policy match criteria

prefix.description: zero or more criteria/filter can be specified to match the term, all criteria have to be met

protocol.description: Routing protocols that can match a switch routing policy term

name.description: Display name of the switch routing policy term

routing_policies.description: Switch routing policies keyed by routing policy name

serial.description: Manufacturer serial number for the switch

site_id.description: Unique identifier of a Mist site

snmp_config.description: SNMP configuration for managed network devices

client_list.description: SNMP client allowlists that can be referenced by communities

client_list_name.description: Name of the SNMP client list

clients.description: SNMP client IP addresses or CIDR ranges allowed by a client list

contact.description: Administrative contact string advertised through SNMP

description.description: Device description string advertised through SNMP

engine_id.description: SNMP engine ID value used for SNMPv3

engine_id_type.description: Method used to derive the SNMP engine ID. enum: `local`, `use_mac_address`

location.description: Physical location string advertised through SNMP

network.description: Management network used for SNMP traffic

categories.description: Trap categories included in an SNMP trap group

group_name.description: Trap group name for this SNMP trap group

targets.description: Trap target addresses for an SNMP trap group

v2c_config.description: SNMPv2c community configuration entries for this SNMP profile

authorization.description: Access level for the SNMPv2c community

client_list_name.description: SNMP client list name referenced by this community

view.description: SNMP view name that must be defined in the views list

v3_config.description: SNMPv3 notification, target, USM, and VACM configuration

notify.description: SNMPv3 notification definitions used for traps and informs

items.description: SNMPv3 notification definition for traps or informs

name.description: Identifier for this SNMPv3 notification definition

tag.description: Notification tag used to select target addresses

type.description: Delivery mode for this SNMPv3 notification, such as trap or inform. enum: `inform`, `trap`

contents.description: OID filter rules in an SNMPv3 notification filter profile

items.description: OID filter rule for an SNMPv3 notification profile

include.description: Whether the matching OID subtree is included

oid.description: Matched OID subtree for this notification filter rule

address.description: IP address or hostname of the SNMP target

address_mask.description: Mask applied to the SNMP target address

port.description: UDP port used by the SNMP target

tag_list.description: Set of notification tags for this target address; use spaces between multiple tags

target_address_name.description: Name of the SNMP target address entry

target_parameters.description: Target parameter profile referenced by this target address

notify_filter.description: Notification filter profile referenced by this target parameter profile

security_name.description: USM security name referenced by this target parameter profile

engine_type.description: SNMP engine type used for this USM configuration. enum: `local_engine`, `remote_engine`

authentication_password.description: Not required if `authentication_type`==`authentication-none`. Include alphabetic, numeric, and special characters, but it cannot include control characters.

authentication_type.description: sha224, sha256, sha384, sha512 are supported in 21.1 and newer release. enum: `authentication-md5`, `authentication-none`, `authentication-sha`, `authentication-sha224`, `authentication-sha256`, `authentication-sha384`, `authentication-sha512`

encryption_password.description: Not required if `encryption_type`==`privacy-none`. Include alphabetic, numeric, and special characters, but it cannot include control characters

name.description: Username for the SNMPv3 USM user

vacm.description: SNMPv3 View-based Access Control Model configuration

items.description: VACM access rule for an SNMP group

prefix_list.description: Context prefix rules for a VACM access entry

items.description: Context prefix rule for a VACM access entry

context_prefix.description: Context prefix for this VACM access rule. Required only if `type`==`context_prefix`

notify_view.description: Notify view name referenced by this VACM access rule

read_view.description: Read view name referenced by this VACM access rule

type.description: VACM context matching type for this access rule. enum: `context_prefix`, `default_context_prefix`

write_view.description: Write view name referenced by this VACM access rule

security_to_group.description: VACM security-name to group mapping configuration

content.description: VACM security-name to group mapping entries

items.description: VACM security-name to group mapping entry

group.description: VACM group name referenced by this mapping

security_name.description: Name of the SNMP security principal mapped to a VACM group

include.description: Whether the root OID is included in this SNMP view

oid.description: Root OID for this SNMP view

view_name.description: Name of the SNMP MIB view definition

bridge_priority.description: Switch STP priority. Range [0, 4k, 8k.. 60k] in steps of 4k. Bridge priority applies to both VSTP and RSTP.

switch_mgmt.description: Switch management-plane access and proxy settings

ap_affinity_threshold.description: AP affinity threshold for switch management. If set in both site settings and organization settings, the site setting value is used.

cli_banner.description: Set Banners for switches. Allows markup formatting

dhcp_option_fqdn.description: Enable to provide the FQDN with DHCP option 81

disable_oob_down_alarm.description: Whether to suppress alarms when the switch out-of-band management interface is down

fips_enabled.description: Whether FIPS mode is enabled on the switch

additionalProperties.description: Local switch user account credentials and access role

password.description: Local password for the switch user account

local_accounts.description: Property key is the user name. For Local user authentication

mxedge_proxy_host.description: IP address or FQDN of the Mist Edge used to proxy the switch management traffic to the Mist Cloud

mxedge_proxy_port.description: Mist Edge port used to proxy the switch management traffic to the Mist Cloud. Value in range 1-65535

protect_re.description:
Restrict inbound-traffic to host
when enabled, all traffic that is not essential to our operation will be dropped 
e.g. ntp / dns / traffic to mist will be allowed by default, if dhcpd is enabled, we'll make sure it works

allowed_services.description: Built-in services explicitly allowed by the Protect RE policy

items.description: Services allowed through protect-RE filters. enum: `icmp`, `ssh`

subnets.description: Source subnets matched by a custom Protect RE ACL

enabled.description:
When enabled, all traffic that is not essential to our operation will be dropped
e.g. ntp / dns / traffic to mist will be allowed by default
     if dhcpd is enabled, we'll make sure it works

hit_count.description: Whether to enable hit count for Protect_RE policy

trusted_hosts.description: Host or subnet entries allowed by the Protect RE policy

radius.description: Switch RADIUS override settings. By default, the switch `radius_config` is used; set `use_different_radius` to use alternate RADIUS settings.

enabled.description: Whether RADIUS is enabled for switch management authentication

radius_config.description: Switch RADIUS authentication and accounting configuration

acct_immediate_update.description: Whether immediate RADIUS accounting updates are sent

acct_interim_interval.description: How frequently should interim accounting be reported, 60-65535. default is 0 (use one specified in Access-Accept request from RADIUS Server). Very frequent messages can affect the performance of the RADIUS server, 600 and up is recommended when enabled

host.description: Address or hostname of the RADIUS accounting server

keywrap_enabled.description: Whether RADIUS keywrap is enabled for messages sent to this accounting server

keywrap_format.description: Encoding format for RADIUS keywrap KEK and MACK values. enum: `ascii`, `hex`

keywrap_mack.description: RADIUS keywrap message authentication code key (MACK)

port.description: RADIUS Auth Port, value from 1 to 65535, default is 1813

secret.description: Shared secret used with this RADIUS accounting server

auth_server_selection.description: Selection strategy for RADIUS authentication servers. enum: `ordered`, `unordered`

host.description: Address or hostname of the RADIUS authentication server

keywrap_enabled.description: Whether RADIUS keywrap is enabled for messages sent to this authentication server

keywrap_format.description: Encoding format for RADIUS keywrap KEK and MACK values. enum: `ascii`, `hex`

keywrap_mack.description: RADIUS keywrap message authentication code key (MACK)

port.description: RADIUS Auth Port, value from 1 to 65535, default is 1812

require_message_authenticator.description: Whether to require Message-Authenticator in requests

secret.description: Shared secret used with this RADIUS authentication server

coa_enabled.description: Whether RADIUS Change of Authorization (CoA) is enabled

coa_port.description: RADIUS CoA Port, value from 1 to 65535, default is 3799

fast_dot1x_timers.description: Whether fast 802.1X timers are enabled for RADIUS authentication

network.description: Use `network`or `source_ip`. Which network the RADIUS server resides, if there's static IP for this network, we'd use it as source-ip

source_ip.description: Use `network` or `source_ip`. Explicit source IP address for RADIUS traffic

use_different_radius.description: Selector for alternate RADIUS settings instead of the default switch `radius_config`

remove_existing_configs.description: By default, only the configuration generated by Mist is cleaned up during the configuration process. If `true`, all the existing configuration will be removed.

root_password.description: Root password for local switch access

tacacs.description: TACACS+ settings for switch management authentication and accounting

host.description: Address or hostname of the TACACS+ accounting server

port.description: TCP port used by the TACACS+ accounting server

secret.description: Shared secret used with this TACACS+ accounting server

timeout.description: TACACS+ accounting server timeout, in seconds

enabled.description: Whether TACACS+ is enabled for switch management authentication

network.description: Source network used for connectivity to the TACACS+ servers

host.description: Address or hostname of the TACACS+ authentication server

port.description: TCP port used by the TACACS+ authentication server

secret.description: Shared secret used with this TACACS+ authentication server

timeout.description: TACACS+ authentication server timeout, in seconds

use_mxedge_proxy.description: Whether to use Mist Edge as a proxy for switch management traffic

use_router_id_as_source_ip.description: Whether to use it for snmp / syslog / tacplus / radius

vars.description: Dictionary of name->value, the vars can then be used in Wlans. This can overwrite those from Site Vars

items.description: Virtual Chassis member identified by MAC address and role

mac.description: Virtual Chassis member MAC address; for FPC0 this matches the device ID MAC

vc_role.description: Both vc_role master and backup will be matched to routing-engine role in Junos preprovisioned VC config. enum: `backup`, `linecard`, `master`

preprovisioned.description: To configure whether the VC is preprovisioned or nonprovisioned

vrf_config.description: VRF enablement settings applied when supported on the device

enabled.description: Whether to enable VRF (when supported on the device)

additionalProperties.description: Switch VRF instance routing and network membership settings
Aggregate route configuration for a network or routing instance

discard.description: Whether to install the aggregate route as a discard route

metric.description: Routing metric assigned to the aggregate route

preference.description: Route preference assigned to the aggregate route

aggregate_routes.description: Property key is the destination subnet (e.g. "172.16.3.0/24")

additionalProperties.description: Aggregate route configuration for a network or routing instance

discard.description: Whether to install the aggregate route as a discard route

metric.description: Routing metric assigned to the aggregate route

preference.description: Route preference assigned to the aggregate route

aggregate_routes6.description: Property key is the destination subnet (e.g. "2a02:1234:420a:10c9::/64")

evpn_auto_loopback_subnet.description: IPv4 subnet used for automatic EVPN loopback addresses in this VRF instance

evpn_auto_loopback_subnet6.description: IPv6 subnet used for automatic EVPN loopback addresses in this VRF instance

additionalProperties.description: Additional IPv4 static route for a VRF instance

via.description: IPv4 next-hop address for this VRF extra route

extra_routes.description: Property key is the destination CIDR (e.g. "10.0.0.0/8")

additionalProperties.description: Additional IPv6 static route for a VRF instance

via.description: IPv6 next-hop address for this VRF extra route

extra_routes6.description: Property key is the destination CIDR (e.g. "2a02:1234:420a:10c9::/64")

networks.description: Unique string values returned or accepted by this schema

vrf_instances.description: Property key is the network name

vrrp_config.description: Junos VRRP configuration applied to a switch or switch profile

additionalProperties.description: VRRP group behavior settings

preempt.description: If `true`, allow preemption (a backup router can preempt a primary router)

priority.description: VRRP priority for this router in the group

groups.description: Property key is the VRRP name

x.description: Horizontal map position of the switch, in pixels

y.description: Vertical map position of the switch, in pixels
